location.origin: Seattle. Acid Tongue is a Seattle folk/indie band led by Guy Keltner. Duwamish ancestral territory. Capitol Hill and the broader Seattle DIY music scene.
location.formative: Seattle. Capitol Hill clubs, house shows, the indie infrastructure. Club-level touring. Self-released through Bandcamp and digital platforms. The Laurel Canyon-meets-Capitol Hill sound.
location.career: Acid Tongue: Seattle folk/indie band. Two albums: Acid Tongue (2017, self-released), Arroyo (2020, self-released). Blends folk, country, and garage rock with loose communal energy. Led by Guy Keltner. Rotating collective membership. E=3.

labels: Self-released (both albums -- Acid Tongue 2017, Arroyo 2020; DIY, Capitol Hill infrastructure; maintains creative control; club-level touring)

members.name: Guy Keltner
members.note: Frontman and creative center. The porch party aesthetic originates with Keltner's communal approach to music-making. Loose collective -- friends show up to play.

associated_musicians.name: Neko Case
associated_musicians.note: Both draw from country and folk traditions. Case is a powerhouse vocalist with precision. Acid Tongue is a communal band with looseness. Both love pedal steel and analog warmth.

associated_musicians.name: Kris Orlowski
associated_musicians.note: Both operate at the club-touring, self-released level. Orlowski is solo and earnest. Acid Tongue is a band and loose. Same DIY infrastructure, different formats.

associated_musicians.name: Pickwick
associated_musicians.note: Both bands known for energetic, communal live shows. Pickwick's energy is soul-revival. Acid Tongue's is folk-rock porch party.

associated_musicians.name: Horse Feathers
associated_musicians.note: Both blend folk and indie. Horse Feathers is quiet chamber folk (E=2). Acid Tongue is communal folk-rock (E=3). Same genre family, opposite registers.

associated_musicians.name: Jesse Sykes
associated_musicians.note: Sykes's folk is dark, atmospheric, cinematic. Acid Tongue's folk is bright, loose, convivial. Two poles of the Seattle folk spectrum.

theory_nodes.concept: The communal jangle (seventy-ninth acoustic element -- the acoustic quality of multiple guitars, pedal steel, loose group harmonies, and a rhythm section that swings rather than drives; the sound of a band having a good time together; not precision performance but collaborative joy; communal warmth through intentional looseness)
examples: Acid Tongue (degree 78) -- multiple guitars (electric and acoustic), pedal steel, loose group harmonies, swinging rhythm. The communal jangle sounds like friends playing on a porch.
Contrast with the ethereal layering (degree 77, Mree) -- solo, studio-crafted intimacy. The communal jangle is group, live-sounding looseness.
Contrast with the communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes) -- tight, rehearsed group singing. The communal jangle is loose, spontaneous.
Contrast with the layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- technology multiplying one voice. The communal jangle is many musicians in a room.
Contrast with the ache (degree 58, Death Cab for Cutie) -- precise emotional targeting. The communal jangle is diffuse emotional warmth.
The communal jangle's beauty comes from looseness, from the gaps between perfect alignment.
theory_nodes.description: The communal jangle is the seventy-ninth acoustic element. Multiple guitars, pedal steel, loose harmonies. Band having a good time together. Collaborative joy. Communal warmth through looseness.

ancestors: The ethereal layering (degree 77, Mree) -- solo studio intimacy. The communal jangle is group live looseness.
The communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes) -- tight group singing. The communal jangle is loose group playing.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-nine acoustic elements across seventy-nine degrees. The communal jangle follows the ethereal layering -- from solo to communal. Track element 80 at degree 79.

theory_nodes.concept: The porch party (seventy-ninth artist-city pattern -- bands whose music sounds like a gathering of friends on a porch, playing for each other rather than an audience; inclusive, warm, slightly loose energy; the anti-spectacle; community as aesthetic substance)
examples: Acid Tongue (degree 78) -- live shows embody porch party energy. Inclusive, warm, communal. The band plays for the room, not for the stage.
Contrast with the YouTube-to-artist pipeline (degree 77, Mree) -- digital-first, solo. The porch party is analog-first, communal.
Contrast with the identity artist (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- identity as substance. The porch party is community as substance.
Contrast with the side project (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- from a bigger act. The porch party is the whole act.
The porch party is not sloppy -- it is intentionally loose. The looseness IS the aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: The porch party is the seventy-ninth artist-city pattern. Music as gathering. Inclusive, warm, loose. Come as you are. Community as aesthetic substance.

ancestors: The YouTube-to-artist pipeline (degree 77, Mree) -- digital audience. The porch party is analog audience.
The identity artist (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- identity substance. The porch party is community substance.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-nine artist-city patterns across seventy-nine degrees. The porch party follows the YouTube pipeline -- from digital to analog. Track pattern 80 at degree 79.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the loose harmony (seventy-eighth deployment; the intentional imperfection in group singing and playing -- voices not quite aligned, guitars slightly out of phase; the beauty in the gap between perfect and actual; the sound of trust between musicians)
examples: Acid Tongue (degree 78) -- the blue note sounds in the looseness of group harmonies. Voices not perfectly aligned. Guitars slightly out of sync. The gap is the beauty.
The loose harmony is the sound of trust -- musicians comfortable enough with each other to not need precision.
Contrast with the delicate suspension (degree 77, Mree) -- fragility vs. resilience. The loose harmony is imperfection vs. joy.
Contrast with the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- two instruments approaching pitch. The loose harmony is many instruments deliberately not quite reaching it.
theory_nodes.description: The loose harmony is the seventy-eighth blue note deployment. Intentional imperfection in group performance. The beauty of the gap between perfect and actual alignment. The sound of trust.

ancestors: Blue note -- the delicate suspension (degree 77, Mree): fragility gap. The loose harmony is precision gap.
Blue note -- the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers): two sources approaching. The loose harmony is many sources not quite meeting.
All seventy-seven prior blue note deployments
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-eight deployments across seventy-nine degrees. Track deployment 79 at degree 79.

theory_nodes.concept: Folk/Indie at Genre Stage 60 -- MILESTONE: sixtieth genre classification; folk warmth meets indie looseness; Laurel Canyon meets Capitol Hill; acoustic guitars and pedal steel alongside garage-rock energy; three consecutive folk-adjacent genres (58-59-60)
examples: Acid Tongue (degree 78) -- Folk/Indie. Genre Stage 60. Milestone.
Three consecutive folk-adjacent: Folk (58/76), Folk/Pop (59/77), Folk/Indie (60/78).
The folk family continues its dominance. Each degree a different register: confessional, ethereal, communal.
Laurel Canyon warmth (harmonies, pedal steel) meets Capitol Hill edge (garage-rock energy, DIY ethic).
theory_nodes.description: Folk/Indie at degree 78 establishes Genre Stage 60 -- a milestone. Laurel Canyon meets Capitol Hill. Three consecutive folk-adjacent genres. Each degree a different register.

ancestors: Folk/Pop at degree 77 (Mree) -- bedroom pop. Acid Tongue's folk/indie adds communal band energy.
Folk at degree 76 (Pureka) -- confessional solo. Three folk-adjacent in a row.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 60 at degree 78. Milestone. Three consecutive folk-adjacent. Track genre at degree 79.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 at degree 78 -- twentieth E=3 deployment; distribution 3:28:20:18:10:2; breaks E=2 streak (76-77); club-touring band level; middle register returns
examples: Acid Tongue (degree 78) -- E=3. Club-touring band. Self-released.
E=3 rises to 20 deployments. Distribution: E1:3, E2:28, E3:20, E4:18, E5:10, E6:2. Total: 81.
Breaks consecutive E=2 streak (76-77). The middle register returns.
Band format generates more acoustic presence than solo E=2 artists, but DIY scale keeps it below E=4.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 at degree 78 is the twentieth E=3 deployment. Distribution: 3:28:20:18:10:2. Breaks E=2 streak. Club-touring band level.

ancestors: E=2 at degree 77 (Mree): consecutive quiet. E=3 breaks the streak.
genealogy.relationship: Twenty E=3 deployments across seventy-nine degrees. Track at degree 79.

theory_nodes.concept: Territorial acknowledgment: Duwamish territory at degree 78 -- Seattle-based band; Duwamish territory continuous; seven territories mapped
examples: Degree 78 -- Duwamish territory (Seattle). Acid Tongue is Seattle-based.
Seven Coast Salish territories remain mapped.
theory_nodes.description: The territorial acknowledgment at degree 78 is Duwamish territory -- Acid Tongue is Seattle-based. Seven territories mapped.

ancestors: Duwamish territory at multiple prior Seattle degrees.
genealogy.relationship: Duwamish territory at degree 78. Seven territories mapped. Track at degree 79.

cross_references.relationship: Alt-country connection. Both draw from country/folk traditions. Case is precision at E=4. Acid Tongue is looseness at E=3. Both love pedal steel and analog warmth.
Working musician. Both operate at club-touring, self-released level. Orlowski is solo and earnest. Acid Tongue is a band and loose. Same DIY infrastructure.
Energetic live. Both bands known for communal live energy. Pickwick's energy is soul-revival. Acid Tongue's is folk-rock porch party.
Indie folk. Both blend folk and indie. Horse Feathers is quiet chamber folk (E=2). Acid Tongue is communal folk-rock (E=3). Same genre family, opposite registers.
Dark folk contrast. Sykes's folk is dark, atmospheric, cinematic. Acid Tongue's is bright, loose, convivial. Two poles of the Seattle folk spectrum.
